rationale: Each of the 41 diseases in the Kaggle dataset is mapped to one of the three exact ASHA-AI care-level strings. Mapping is conservative — ambiguous diseases (e.g. Pneumonia, Asthma, Malaria) default to Clinic Visit; only diseases with consistently life-threatening presentations map to Emergency Room. Mild, self-limiting, or chronic-stable conditions map to Home Care. The Plan 2.0 rule layer (R1-R9 from RED_FLAGS.md) overrides this mapping when red-flag symptoms are present at inference time — see METHODOLOGY.md §P1.1.
notes: Kaggle disease names include trailing whitespace and typos verbatim (e.g. 'Diabetes ', 'Osteoarthristis', 'Peptic ulcer diseae'). Map keys preserve them so DataFrame .map() succeeds without preprocessing.

Hypoglycemia is mapped to Emergency Room conservatively — severe hypoglycemia (altered consciousness, seizure) is ESI Level 1; mild presentations would clinically be Home Care with oral glucose. The classifier sees symptom multi-hot vectors, not severity grading, so the conservative bucket is correct for safety.
Bronchial Asthma maps to Clinic Visit; the R7 red-flag rule (cannot_speak_full_sentences, SpO2<92) elevates severe presentations to Emergency Room at inference time.
Malaria, Dengue, Pneumonia map to Clinic Visit; if vitals + sepsis-qSOFA criteria are met at inference, R4 escalates to Emergency Room.
